mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicate robust economic activity and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of months where PAYEMS increases by more than 200k, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When real personal spending exceeds 450,000 million dollars, aggregate demand outpaces productive capacity, triggering inflation expectations and monetary tightening fears that produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in >70% of months where RSAFS crosses the 450,000 threshold, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index below 100.0 signals a supply contraction that forces central banks to deploy aggressive liquidity measures, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >65% of months where INDPRO falls below 100.0, or the predictive coefficient turns insignificant (p > 0.10) in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of unemployment above 5.0% and a federal funds rate below 0.5% creates a stagflationary regime where weak labor demand offsets cheap money, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between UNRATE and FEDFUNDS yields a positive coefficient in a multivariate regression, or 20-day returns are positive in >60% of joint-trigger months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous CPIAUCSL levels above 250.0 and a federal funds rate below 1.0% compress real interest rates into deeply negative territory, eroding equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where both conditions hold, or the interaction effect fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above 3.0% confirms robust aggregate demand that translates into higher corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.0%, or the signal reverses direction during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: During a supply-constrained regime, rising industrial production indicates capacity bottlenecks and input cost inflation that compress corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where INDPRO rises during a supply-constrained regime, or the mechanism fails to hold when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.
